method: Native GoHighLevel platform research (Call Connect feature docs, multi-ring phone number docs, round-robin Assign to User action) applied to the existing '02. Lead Form NoBook 5m AI Call SMS' draft workflow (id c70c6933-a744-4201-b41b-bf4d46df3b50) from the 2026-07-22 live re-map.
headline: GHL already has a native feature built for exactly this: Call Connect. A workflow Call step rings a staff member (or a shared number), plays a whisper message, and on any keypress immediately bridges an outbound call to the lead -- that IS the 'ring staff, then convert to outbound' pattern. Paired with the native round-robin Assign to User action, this becomes a real SDR queue without any custom telephony build. Recommendation: round-robin Assign to User, then Call Connect, with the existing AI-call/SMS fallback kept as the safety net when no rep answers.

mechanics.title: How Call Connect works end to end

steps.detail: A call is placed to the assigned user's phone, or to the sub-account's business phone number if no user is assigned.
The rep hears a short message before being connected -- lead source, lead name via merge fields, and the literal phrase 'press any key to connect' (required wording).
GHL immediately places the outbound call to the lead's phone number and bridges both lines -- functionally the inbound-then-outbound conversion.

steps.what: If the rep does not answer
steps.detail: The workflow continues to its next step -- this is the fallback branch point where the existing AI-call/SMS sequence should sit.

steps.what: If the rep answers but never presses a key
steps.detail: The whisper message repeats up to 3 times, then GHL automatically places the call to the lead anyway.

mechanics.prerequisite: Call Connect must be explicitly enabled per phone number: Settings -> Phone Numbers -> (3-dot menu) -> Edit Configuration -> toggle 'Enable Call Connect Feature'.

options.name: Round-robin Assign to User, then Call Connect
options.how_it_works: An Assign to User action (round-robin mode across the SDR list) runs first, distributing the lead to one specific rep. The Call step then rings that assigned user's phone directly.
pros: Fair, automatic load-balancing across the SDR team with zero manual queue management.
One rep owns the lead end to end -- clear accountability for follow-through after the call.
Matches GHL's own documented 'Fast Five' recipe -- no custom build, just two stock actions in sequence.
Assignment persists on the contact/opportunity record for reporting (who called whom).
cons: If the assigned rep is unavailable (lunch, on another call), the lead waits for that one ring cycle before falling through to the fallback branch -- no automatic retry to a second rep in the same pass.
Requires the SDR team roster to be kept current in the round-robin assignment pool.

options.name: Multi-ring shared number, then Call Connect
options.how_it_works: The SDR team's shared line is configured as a multi-ring number (up to 10 users ring simultaneously, first to answer wins). The Call step points at that shared business number instead of an assigned user.
pros: Fastest possible pickup -- whichever rep is free answers immediately, no single-rep bottleneck.
Simple to set up: one phone-number configuration change, no workflow assignment logic needed.
cons: No built-in ownership -- whoever happens to answer gets the lead, which can concentrate volume on the fastest responder rather than balancing the team.
Weaker reporting: harder to attribute call outcomes to a specific rep without an extra manual disposition step.
Every free rep's phone rings for every no-book lead, which can be disruptive if volume is high.

options.name: Literal inbound-ring-group then separate outbound leg (custom build)
options.how_it_works: The idea as originally proposed: place a true inbound call into a ring group, then once a rep answers, originate a brand new outbound call leg to the lead and bridge it manually.
pros: Conceptually clean 1:1 match to a traditional call-center ring-group-to-outbound pattern.
cons: Not how GHL's native Call action works -- it does not expose a raw 'ring group then originate new outbound leg' primitive; Call Connect already collapses this into one whispered call + keypress bridge.

Would require a custom telephony integration (e.g., a separate IVR/PBX or a third-party call-routing API) outside GHL to replicate literally, adding cost and a new system to maintain for no behavioral gain over Call Connect.

recommended_build.title: Recommended build: slot round-robin Call Connect in front of the existing draft
recommended_build.target_workflow: 02. Lead Form NoBook 5m AI Call SMS
recommended_build.current_shape: Tag wf:no-book-5m -> Add task -> Update cf_cc_call_result -> Send Fallback SMS -> tag/field bookkeeping -> Wait 5 min -> Wait 3 min -> a few unresolved decision branches. The 'AI call' step itself is a placeholder in the current draft.

proposed_steps.detail: Form Submitted (no-book) OR Contact Tag Added: source_no_book -- wire this now; this is the only missing piece keeping the workflow from ever firing.
New step. Round-robin across the live SDR list. This replaces/precedes the current 'Add task' step -- keep the task creation too, as a CRM record of the attempt.

New step. Rings the just-assigned rep's phone. Whisper message: "New 5-minute no-book lead, {{contact.first_name}}, from {{contact.attributionSource}}. Press any key to connect." Requires Call Connect enabled on the number used.

proposed_steps.action: If/Else: did the rep answer + press a key?
proposed_steps.detail: New branch. GHL's Call step naturally falls through to the next workflow step if unanswered -- use that fall-through as the 'no answer' path rather than a manual condition check.

proposed_steps.action: Answered branch -> End (or a short delay then a task to log outcome)
proposed_steps.detail: Update cf_cc_call_result to 'Connected - Live Rep', exit the workflow -- the rep is now on the phone with the lead.

Keep the current draft's steps exactly as built: AI call attempt, then Send Fallback SMS with the wait timers already in place (Wait 5 min, Wait 3 min) and the existing tag bookkeeping (wf:pending-ai-call, wf:sms-fallback-sent).

compliance_notes: The existing 'Send Fallback SMS' step already includes 'Reply STOP to opt out.' -- keep this exact language, it is the only STOP-compliant message in the current draft and should not be lost when the workflow is extended.

The whisper message is internal (rep-only, never heard by the lead) so it is not subject to the same compliance language as member-facing SMS/email, but should still avoid PHI per the account's HIPAA-safe SMS content rule in case it is ever logged or transcribed.

Quiet hours (8am-9pm contact timezone) should gate the Call step exactly as they gate every other SMS/call step in the account -- do not let the round-robin Call Connect step fire outside that window; route straight to the SMS fallback instead.
rollout_plan: 1. Enable Call Connect on the phone number this workflow will use (Settings -> Phone Numbers -> Edit Configuration).
2. Add the Assign to User (round-robin) step with the current SDR roster.
3. Add the Call (Call Connect) step with the whisper message above.
4. Add the If/Else fall-through wiring so unanswered calls continue into the existing AI-call/SMS steps unchanged.
5. Test with 2 phones (rep + lead) per GHL's own testing guidance before publishing.
6. Wire the trigger and publish as a draft-tested workflow, per the account's build-extract-diff verification loop -- nothing publishes without explicit approval.
